I found relevant information about the Bundesbank BISTA XML system and its test facility upload portal. Here are the key findings:

## Banking Statistics (BISTA) XML System

ExtraNet is the e-business platform of the Deutsche Bundesbank.

### BISTA File Naming and Format

The file names comprise the operational area, the reporting deadline and a file extension. The operational area is specified using a four-digit [five-digit when technically possible] abbreviation. The format for the reporting deadline is YYMM. The file extension is ".txt".

An example of a BISTA file is "bista1411.xml: Bilanzstatistiken inländischer Banken für den Meldemonat November 2014 im XML-Format" (Balance sheet statistics of domestic banks for November 2014 in XML format).

### Test File Submission

The file name for test files must begin with "TEST_", both in ZIP and XML files.

### Registration and Testing Process

If you want to submit reports via ExtraNet, you must first register. Based on your registration request, you will be informed about the required test submission. After successful test submission, the deadline for regular submission via ExtraNet is agreed upon bilaterally.

### Electronic Reporting (Meldewesen)

The document describes the bank statistical file formats accepted by Deutsche Bundesbank's XML-based electronic reporting system (XMW). The focus is on specialist content; technical details are presented in simplified form. More detailed documentation (XML Schema files) are available separately.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

results:
I found relevant information about the Bundesbank BISTA XML system and its test facility upload portal. Here are the key findings:

## Banking Statistics (BISTA) XML System

ExtraNet is the e-business platform of the Deutsche Bundesbank.

### BISTA File Naming and Format

The file names comprise the operational area, the reporting deadline and a file extension. The operational area is specified using a four-digit [five-digit when technically possible] abbreviation. The format for the reporting deadline is YYMM. The file extension is ".txt".

An example of a BISTA file is "bista1411.xml: Bilanzstatistiken inländischer Banken für den Meldemonat November 2014 im XML-Format" (Balance sheet statistics of domestic banks for November 2014 in XML format).

### Test File Submission

The file name for test files must begin with "TEST_", both in ZIP and XML files.

### Registration and Testing Process

If you want to submit reports via ExtraNet, you must first register. Based on your registration request, you will be informed about the required test submission. After successful test submission, the deadline for regular submission via ExtraNet is agreed upon bilaterally.

### Electronic Reporting (Meldewesen)

The document describes the bank statistical file formats accepted by Deutsche Bundesbank's XML-based electronic reporting system (XMW). The focus is on specialist content; technical details are presented in simplified form. More detailed documentation (XML Schema files) are available separately.

content.text:
Yes — the Bundesbank does support test submissions for BISTA XML. Here's what I found:

**Test file submission via ExtraNet / NExt:**

- Test files must have names beginning with `TEST_` (applies to both ZIP and XML filenames, e.g. `TEST_bista2411.xml`)
- To submit via [ExtraNet](https://www.bundesbank.de/de/service/extranet), you first register, and then a **mandatory test submission** is required before going live — the go-live date is agreed bilaterally after the test passes

**Relevant portals:**
- [ExtraNet / NExt (Bankenstatistik)](https://www.bundesbank.de/de/service/meldewesen/bankenstatistik/extranet-next/bankenstatistik-extranet-next-611396) — the main submission portal
- [NExt (Neukonzeption ExtraNet)](https://www.bundesbank.de/de/service/meldewesen/aussenwirtschaft/elektronische-einreichung/next-960348) — the newer successor platform

**In short:** there isn't a separate open sandbox, but the `TEST_` prefix convention routes submissions as test data within the same portal, and a formal test run is part of the onboarding process before production reporting begins.

Search the web for information about whether the Deutsche Bundesbank (German central bank) has a test facility or sandbox environment where banks can upload test BISTA XML files (Monatliche Bilanzstatistik / Monthly Balance Sheet Statistics). Look for:
1. Any official Bundesbank test portal or staging environment for BISTA XML submissions
2. The ExtraNet portal or similar submission systems
3. Any documentation about testing BISTA XML before production submission
4. URLs or portal names for Bundesbank reporting test systems

Please search and return what you find.

content.thinking: The user wants me to store the BISTA test facility information in a file called test.md in the current working directory.

input.content:
# Bundesbank BISTA XML Test Facility

## Test File Submission

- Test files must have names beginning with `TEST_` (applies to both ZIP and XML filenames)
  - Example: `TEST_bista2411.xml`
- Submitted via the same portal as production — the `TEST_` prefix routes them as test data

## Onboarding / Go-Live Process

1. Register with ExtraNet or NExt
2. A mandatory test submission is required before going live
3. Go-live date is agreed bilaterally with Bundesbank after the test passes

## Relevant Portals

- [ExtraNet / NExt (Bankenstatistik)](https://www.bundesbank.de/de/service/meldewesen/bankenstatistik/extranet-next/bankenstatistik-extranet-next-611396) — main submission portal
- [NExt (Neukonzeption ExtraNet)](https://www.bundesbank.de/de/service/meldewesen/aussenwirtschaft/elektronische-einreichung/next-960348) — newer successor platform
- [ExtraNet general](https://www.bundesbank.de/de/service/extranet)

## Notes

- No separate open sandbox environment; testing happens within the production portal using the `TEST_` naming convention
- XML format documentation: [xml-format-descriptions-data.pdf](https://www.bundesbank.de/resource/blob/762746/ffeb9ce2090a9359b6a4aaba2cfabfa3/mL/xml-format-descriptions-data.pdf)
